location.office_time_required: four days a week in the office, Fridays from home

level_signals: 6–10 years in strategy, operations, consulting, or program management
Title is 'Principal', denoting a senior individual contributor
Comp range $150,000–$208,000 base consistent with Principal IC at a late-stage FinTech
Described as 'high-visibility, high-impact individual contributor role'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6–10 years in strategy, operations, consulting, or program management in a fast-paced, matrixed environment.
Excellent time management and prioritization skills; able to manage multiple complex workstreams simultaneously.
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to tailor messaging for different audiences and influence at all levels.
Skilled at listening, building rapport, and cultivating strong partnerships across teams.
Ability to zoom out to see the big picture, while quickly connecting it to actionable execution.
Confident navigating change and bringing structure to open-ended problems.
Proven track record of independently managing complex, cross-functional projects from concept to completion.
Strong ability to structure problems, identify root causes, and recommend solutions.
Comfort working with data to conduct analyses, synthesize insights, and make strategic recommendations.
Experience working with or supporting People-related functions (e.g., HR, Org Design, Talent) required.

scope.surface: People Team strategic programs and cross-functional initiatives across the enterprise
primary_metrics_inferred: Stakeholder alignment and adoption of People initiatives
Quality and timeliness of strategic analyses informing People decisions
core_responsibilities: Lead and execute complex, cross-functional initiatives that directly support the People Team's most important strategic goals
Partner closely with leaders to define problems, structure initiatives, and conduct quantitative and qualitative analysis to inform decisions
Own end-to-end planning and delivery of strategic programs
Support the development and facilitation of operating cadences, such as quarterly planning, strategy reviews, and leadership team check-ins
Build trusted relationships across People Team leaders and cross-functional partners
Co-create thoughtful communication and change enablement strategies that support initiative success, team alignment, and adoption
Actively listen and adapt to the evolving needs and culture of the organization

green_flags: Equity and bonus mentioned in addition to base salary
Generous benefits including up to 24 weeks paid parental leave and $15K family planning reimbursement
Clear reporting structure (Director, Head of People Strategy & Programs)
High-visibility, Principal-level IC role with direct impact on enterprise goals
Role scope is well-defined with specific responsibility areas enumerated
red_flags: JD hashtag #LI-Onsite suggests fully onsite, but benefits section describes a 4-day in-office / 1-day remote hybrid policy — mild inconsistency worth confirming
No specific tools or systems named, making it harder to assess technical expectations
Visa sponsorship not addressed; unclear for international candidates
No remote option indicated for candidates outside office locations

Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; work_auth set to 'open' and visa_sponsorship set to 'unclear'.
